notes_for_human_reviewer: TITLE-CHASER FAILURE MODE — the entitlement direction. Wants the title and pay as an earned reward ('I've earned the step up', 'tired of doing the work without the seniority or comp', 'others I started with have already made PM') with zero honest self-assessment — honest_self_assessment FLOORS at 1. Explicitly treats PM as a promotion and assumes observation equals readiness ('I don't think I need a load of development... I've watched how it's done') — role_change_understanding FLOORS at 1 (the necessary-but-not-sufficient error in its purest form). No real development plan (development_plan 2), agency present but as entitlement/ultimatum not ownership of costs (agency 2), and the framing is a demand-with-threat not a development conversation (professional_framing 2). Tests whether the grader catches title-chasing as the failure the chapter warns about — wanting the rung, not the role. Soft dims pre-set {2, tol 2}; the two floors hold it off pass.
